irregularities.label: MULTI_LEVEL_HEADER
irregularities.evidence: Headers for data columns span two rows: row 0 contains language labels ("french-language workers", "english-language workers") and row 1 contains "percent" indicating the measure.
irregularities.details: The header depth is 3 rows, with rows 0 and 1 providing hierarchical information for the numeric columns, forming a two-level header structure.

irregularities.label: WIDE_FORMAT
irregularities.evidence: Column headers in row 0 are values of the variable "language" (e.g., "french-language workers", "english-language workers") rather than variable names. These headers can be collapsed into a single "language" column without losing information.
irregularities.details: The data columns represent percentages for different language groups, spreading the language variable across multiple columns. Headers are dimension values, not variable names.

irregularities.label: SECTION_HEADER_ROWS
irregularities.evidence: Row 6 in the data region has "religion" in column 0 with empty numeric columns, acting as a section header for the subsequent religion data rows (rows 7 to 15).
irregularities.details: This non-data row divides the data into two sections ("place of birth" and "religion") and applies to all rows until the next section header, with no numeric values of its own.
